summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A car worth $30,000 depreciates at 15% per year. What is its value after 2 years?

V = 30000(0.85)^2 = 30000(0.7225) = $21,675.

In y = 1000(0.92)^t, what is the decay rate?

Decay factor = 0.92 = 1 - 0.08. The decay rate is 0.08, or 8%.

A medication's concentration in the bloodstream decreases by 25% each hour. Starting at 400 mg, what is the concentration after 3 hours?

C(t) = 400(0.75)^t. C(3) = 400(0.75)^3 = 400(0.421875) = 168.75 mg.

A computer loses 20% of its value each year. It was purchased for $1,200. What is its value after 4 years?

V = 1200(0.80)^4 = 1200(0.4096) = $491.52.

The function f(t) = 5000(0.5)^t models a decay process. What is f(0)?

f(0) = 5000(0.5)^0 = 5000(1) = 5,000. The initial value is always a.

What is exponential decay?

Exponential decay occurs when a quantity decreases by a constant percentage each time period, multiplied by a factor between 0 and 1.

What is a 'decay factor'?

The decay factor b is between 0 and 1 and represents the fraction of the quantity that remains after each time period.

What is 'half-life'?

Half-life is the time required for a quantity to decay to half of its current value. It is a constant for any given exponential decay process.

A new smartphone costs $1,000 and loses 35% of its value each year. What is the phone worth after 3 years?

V = 1000(0.65)^3 = 1000(0.274625) = $274.63.

A cup of coffee at 200 degrees F cools at a rate of 10% per minute. What is the temperature after 5 minutes?

T(t) = 200(0.90)^t. T(5) = 200(0.90)^5 = 200(0.59049) = 118.10 degrees F.
